message:
feat(tui): implement mouse drag-and-drop message rethreading across screens

Adds a complete drag-and-drop interaction for moving messages between
threads, addressing a long-standing UX gap where rethreading required
manual thread ID entry or CLI commands.

The interaction model uses a 200ms hold delay before activating the drag
(preventing accidental drags from clicks), renders a ghost label at the
cursor position showing the dragged message subject, and provides visual
feedback through border color and row highlighting on valid/invalid drop
targets.

Implementation spans three layers:

- tui_bridge.rs: New MessageDragSnapshot struct and shared state methods
  (set/get/clear) allow the drag state to be coordinated across screens.
  The snapshot includes message identity, source thread, cursor position,
  and hover validation state.

- tui_screens/messages.rs and tui_screens/threads.rs: Both screens gain
  full drag lifecycle handling (Pending -> Active -> finish/cancel) with
  hit-testing against their respective list areas. The messages screen
  initiates drags from the results list; the threads screen initiates
  from the detail panel's selected message. Drop targets are thread rows
  in either screen's list, validated to reject same-thread drops.

- tui_app.rs: New execute_rethread_message() performs the actual SQLite
  UPDATE (thread_id reassignment) synchronously, with error handling and
  toast notifications. The rethread_message operation is dispatched via
  ActionExecute messages from the drag completion. A ghost overlay
  (render_message_drag_ghost) is drawn at the app level so it persists
  across screen boundaries.

- tui_keymap.rs: Import order normalized by rustfmt (no functional
  change).

All new code paths include unit tests for argument parsing, invalid
input rejection, and dispatch error handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
